1→# Express Postgres Starter
     2→
     3→A minimal Express.js template with PostgreSQL connection, designed for Polsia infrastructure provisioning.
     4→
     5→## Requirements
     6→
     7→- Node.js 18+
     8→- PostgreSQL database (Neon recommended)
     9→
    10→## Environment Variables
    11→
    12→- `DATABASE_URL` - PostgreSQL connection string (required)
    13→- `PORT` - Server port (default: 3000)
    14→
    15→## Endpoints
    16→
    17→- `GET /` - Hello message
    18→- `GET /health` - Health check (verifies database connection)
    19→
    20→## Local Development
    21→
    22→```bash
    23→npm install
    24→DATABASE_URL="postgresql://..." npm run dev
    25→```
    26→
    27→## Deployment
    28→
    29→This template is configured for Render deployment via `render.yaml`.
    30→

1→# Rekrut AI — Competitive Analysis: AI Capabilities Across 16 Modules
     2→
     3→**Date:** February 9, 2026
     4→**Purpose:** Map the AI capabilities landscape across every Rekrut AI module, identify where competitors are genuinely AI-native vs. marketing fluff, and define where Rekrut AI can leapfrog.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Executive Summary
     9→
    10→1. **No competitor offers a unified, AI-native hiring platform across all 16 modules.** Every player is strong in 2-3 areas and weak everywhere else. Rekrut AI's opportunity is being the first truly end-to-end AI-native platform.
    11→2. **OmniScore (two-sided scoring) has zero direct competitors.** Glassdoor does employer ratings. ATS platforms do candidate scoring. Nobody combines both into a single scoring system inside the hiring workflow.
    12→3. **Most "AI-powered" claims are marketing labels.** Real AI (ML-trained models, generative AI, agentic workflows) exists at HireVue, Workday, Deel, and LinkedIn. Everyone else is keyword matching with a chatbot bolted on.
    13→4. **The compliance moat is real.** NYC LL144, EU AI Act (high-risk deadline August 2026), and California's ADS regulations create massive barriers. Platforms that bake in compliance win enterprise deals.
    14→5. **Global payroll is the most AI-advanced category.** Deel ($17.3B valuation) and Papaya Global are leading with agentic AI agents. Rekrut AI needs strong partnerships or deep investment here.
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Strategic Priorities
    19→
    20→1. **OmniScore** — No competitor has two-sided scoring. Build first, market loudly.
    21→2. **AI-Native Assessment + Matching** — Own the full pipeline from skills test to job match with real ML.
    22→3. **Compliance as a Feature** — EU AI Act deadline Aug 2026. Built-in bias auditing = enterprise sales.
    23→4. **Global Payroll via Partnership** — Integrate Deel/Remote/Papaya APIs, don't build from scratch.
    24→5. **AI Interview Coaching (Both Sides)** — Coach candidates AND evaluate interviewers in one system.
    25→
    26→## Key Finding: AI-Native vs AI-Bolted-On
    27→
    28→Every major competitor added AI as an afterthought. HireVue (founded 2004, AI ~2016), Greenhouse (2012, AI 2024), Workday (2005, acquired HiredScore 2024). Only Deel (2019) approaches AI-native in the payroll space. Rekrut AI, building in 2026, has the unique opportunity to design every module with AI at the core from day one.
    29→
    30→---
    31→
    32→## Module-by-Module Competitive Landscape
    33→
    34→### 1. AI Skill Assessments
    35→| Competitor | AI Depth | Notes |
    36→|------------|----------|-------|
    37→| **Codility** | Medium | Auto-grading, plagiarism detection. No adaptive difficulty. |
    38→| **TestGorilla** | Low | Template-based tests. "AI" is just scoring rules. |
    39→| **HackerRank** | Medium | CodeSignal has adaptive testing. HackerRank catching up. |
    40→| **Rekrut AI** | **High** | Adaptive difficulty, AI-generated questions, real-time feedback. |
    41→
    42→### 2. AI Interview Coaching
    43→| Competitor | AI Depth | Notes |
    44→|------------|----------|-------|
    45→| **HireVue** | High | Video analysis, facial coding (controversial). Enterprise-only. |
    46→| **LinkedIn** | Low | Tips articles. No real-time coaching. |
    47→| **Rekrut AI** | **High** | Video mock interviews, body language analysis, speech metrics, real-time coaching. |
    48→
    49→### 3. AI Profile Matching
    50→| Competitor | AI Depth | Notes |
    51→|------------|----------|-------|
    52→| **LinkedIn Recruiter** | High | Massive data moat. ML-powered recommendations. |
    53→| **Workday** | High | HiredScore acquisition (2024). Enterprise matching. |
    54→| **Indeed** | Medium | Resume matching but limited AI depth. |
    55→| **Rekrut AI** | **Medium-High** | Semantic matching engine, weighted scoring. Needs data scale. |
    56→
    57→### 4. OmniScore (Two-Sided)
    58→| Competitor | AI Depth | Notes |
    59→|------------|----------|-------|
    60→| **Glassdoor** | N/A | Employer ratings only. Separate from hiring. |
    61→| **ATS platforms** | Low | Candidate scoring only. One-sided. |
    62→| **Rekrut AI** | **UNIQUE** | Two-sided scoring (candidate + company). Zero direct competitors. |
    63→
    64→### 5. AI Document Management
    65→| Competitor | AI Depth | Notes |
    66→|------------|----------|-------|
    67→| **Checkr** | Medium | Background checks. Good verification, narrow scope. |
    68→| **Greenhouse** | Low | File uploads. No AI extraction. |
    69→| **Rekrut AI** | **High** | AI extraction, verification, authenticity checks. |
    70→
    71→### 6-15. Remaining Modules Summary
    72→
    73→| Module | Best Competitor | Their AI Level | Rekrut AI Edge |
    74→|--------|----------------|----------------|----------------|
    75→| Hiring Dashboard | Greenhouse/Lever | Low-Medium | AI-powered pipeline insights |
    76→| Automated Scheduler | Calendly | None (rule-based) | AI optimal time selection |
    77→| Real-time Collaboration | Slack integrations | None | AI-assisted team decisions |
    78→| AI Feedback Loop | HireVue | Medium | Full-cycle feedback, both sides |
    79→| Custom Workflow | Greenhouse | Low | AI-suggested workflows |
    80→| Performance Analytics | Workday | Medium | Predictive analytics |
    81→| Candidate Experience | Indeed | Low | Personalized AI guidance |
    82→| Compliance & Security | Checkr | Medium | EU AI Act built-in |
    83→| Job Recommendations | LinkedIn | High | Niche: AI-native context |
    84→| Onboarding | BambooHR | Low | Full AI automation |
    85→
    86→### 16. Global Payroll
    87→| Competitor | AI Depth | Notes |
    88→|------------|----------|-------|
    89→| **Deel** | Very High | $17.3B. Agentic AI for compliance. Market leader. |
    90→| **Remote.com** | High | AI contractor classification. Strong. |
    91→| **Papaya Global** | High | AI-powered payroll processing. |
    92→| **Oyster HR** | Medium | Growing but behind on AI. |
    93→| **Rekrut AI** | **Medium** | Built in-house (US + India). Partnership recommended. |
    94→

    96→
    97→## Competitors Analyzed (14)
    98→
    99→HireVue, Codility, TestGorilla, Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, LinkedIn Recruiter, Indeed, Calendly, Checkr, Glassdoor, Deel, Remote.com, Papaya Global

1→# Rekrut AI — Gap Analysis
     2→
     3→**Date:** February 10, 2026
     4→**Sources:** MODULE_AUDIT.md (Feb 9) + COMPETITIVE_ANALYSIS.md (Feb 9) + codebase inspection
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## TL;DR
     9→
    10→Rekrut AI has **13 of 15 original modules fully built** with real backend logic and frontend UIs. The codebase is large (10K+ lines of routes, 29 migrations, 10 services). But "built" ≠ "competitive." Cross-referencing with the competitive analysis reveals significant gaps in depth, polish, and missing competitive features.
    11→
    12→**Biggest strategic gap:** OmniScore is the #1 differentiator (zero competitors) but the implementation is only 160 lines. That's the priority.
    13→
    14→---
    15→
    16→## 1. Fully Built & Working
    17→
    18→These modules have real backend routes, database tables, AI integration, and frontend UIs on both candidate and recruiter sides.
    19→
    20→| Module | Route Size | Frontend | AI Depth | Competitive Position |
    21→|--------|-----------|----------|----------|---------------------|
    22→| **AI Skill Assessments** | 980 lines | React + legacy | Adaptive questions, scoring, feedback | Ahead of TestGorilla, on par with Codility |
    23→| **AI Interview Coaching** | 913 lines | React + legacy (73K tsx) | Video analysis, body language, speech metrics | On par with HireVue (minus enterprise scale) |
    24→| **AI Profile Matching** | 206 lines + matching-engine service | React | Semantic matching, weighted scoring | Behind LinkedIn (data moat), ahead of most ATS |
    25→| **AI Document Management** | 422 lines + doc-verification service | Legacy HTML | AI extraction, verification, authenticity | Ahead of Greenhouse, on par with Checkr |
    26→| **Hiring Dashboard** | 754 lines (recruiter.js) | React + legacy | Pipeline, analytics, status tracking | Competitive with Greenhouse/Lever |
    27→| **AI Feedback Loop** | Part of interviews.js | React | Analysis of responses, coaching recs | Unique: both-sides approach |
    28→| **Performance Analytics** | 124 lines | Legacy HTML (recruiter-analytics) | Assessment + interview metrics | Basic — Workday is far ahead |
    29→| **Candidate Ranking** | Part of matching.js | React | Semantic ranking | Functional, needs scale data |
    30→| **Personalized Candidate Experience** | 1416 lines (candidate.js) | React (7 pages) | Tailored dashboard, personalized flow | Ahead of most ATS platforms |
    31→| **Compliance & Security** | 553 lines + biasDetection service | Legacy HTML | Background checks, legal tracking, bias detection | Good foundation. EU AI Act gap (see below) |
    32→| **Job Recommendations** | Part of matching.js | React | Smart recommendations | Behind LinkedIn, ahead of Indeed |
    33→| **Onboarding** | 2364 lines (largest route!) | React (97K tsx!) + legacy (52K) | AI-driven paperwork, doc generation, E2E flow | Ahead of BambooHR. Strongest module. |
    34→| **Global Payroll** | 941 lines + payroll-calculator service | React (45K tsx) + legacy | US + India tax calculations, multi-country | Behind Deel/Remote. Partnership recommended. |
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## 2. Partially Built (Needs Completion)
    39→
    40→### Real-time Collaboration Tools
    41→- **Current state:** Room creation endpoints in recruiter.js, chat signal references
    42→- **Missing:** No WebSocket server (server.js has no socket.io/ws setup). No live presence. No real-time messaging.
    43→- **Competitive context:** Most ATS platforms use Slack integrations. Real-time is a differentiator IF built.
    44→- **Effort:** Medium (2-3 days). Add socket.io to server.js, build chat UI.
    45→- **Priority:** LOW — Slack integration would deliver 80% of value at 20% of effort.
    46→
    47→### Customizable Hiring Workflow
    48→- **Current state:** Job stages exist in jobs.js. Recruiter can create jobs with stages.
    49→- **Missing:** No custom stage creation UI. No drag-and-drop pipeline builder. No workflow templates.
    50→- **Competitive context:** Greenhouse and Lever are strong here. Table stakes for enterprise.
    51→- **Effort:** Medium-High (3-5 days). Pipeline builder UI + backend stage CRUD.
    52→- **Priority:** MEDIUM — Important for enterprise, but not a differentiator.
    53→
    54→### React SPA Migration (Dual Frontend)
    55→- **Current state:** React SPA exists with 23 pages (12 candidate, 11 recruiter). But 39 legacy HTML files still in public/.
    56→- **Missing:** Legacy HTML pages are the primary frontend for many modules (compliance, documents, analytics, company profile). React versions don't exist for all.
    57→- **Competitive context:** Not user-facing, but affects development speed and consistency.
    58→- **Effort:** High (1-2 weeks). Migrate remaining legacy pages to React.
    59→- **Priority:** MEDIUM — Technical debt. Blocks rapid iteration.
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## 3. Missing Entirely
    64→
    65→These capabilities don't exist in the codebase but are critical based on competitive analysis.
    66→
    67→### 🔴 HIGH PRIORITY — Build Now
    68→
    69→| Gap | Why It Matters | Competitive Benchmark | Effort |
    70→|-----|---------------|----------------------|--------|
    71→| **OmniScore depth** | #1 differentiator with ZERO competitors. Current implementation is 160 lines — needs to be 10x deeper with company scoring, historical trends, explainability. | Nobody does this. Own the category. | 3-5 days |
    72→| **EU AI Act compliance module** | Deadline August 2026. Bias auditing exists (biasDetection.js) but no formal audit trail, no risk classification, no transparency reports. | Checkr and HireVue are scrambling. First-mover advantage. | 3-5 days |
    73→| **ATS/HRIS integrations** | No integrations with Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, BambooHR. Enterprise buyers need this. | Every competitor has integration marketplaces. | 5-10 days (per integration) |
    74→| **Email notifications** | No transactional email system. Candidates/recruiters get no notifications for applications, offers, interviews. | Every competitor has this. Table stakes. | 2-3 days |
    75→
    76→### 🟡 MEDIUM PRIORITY — Build Soon
    77→
    78→| Gap | Why It Matters | Competitive Benchmark | Effort |
    79→|-----|---------------|----------------------|--------|
    80→| **Interviewer evaluation** | Competitive analysis says coach both sides. Currently only coaches candidates. | HireVue does this for enterprise. | 2-3 days |
    81→| **AI-suggested job descriptions** | Recruiter creates jobs manually. AI should generate/optimize descriptions. | LinkedIn and Indeed offer this. | 1-2 days |
    82→| **Candidate self-service portal** | Candidates can't check application status independently (no status page). | Greenhouse, Lever offer this. | 1-2 days |
    83→| **Mobile responsiveness** | Unknown state across legacy HTML pages. React pages likely responsive (Tailwind). | All competitors are mobile-first. | 2-4 days audit + fix |
    84→| **Pricing page** | pricing.html is 577 bytes (empty shell). No actual pricing flow. | Every SaaS competitor has this. | 1 day |
    85→| **Automated scheduler (calendar integration)** | Interview scheduling exists but no Google Calendar/Outlook integration. Manual scheduling defeats the purpose. | Calendly sets the bar. | 3-5 days |
    86→
    87→### 🟢 LOW PRIORITY — Nice to Have
    88→
    89→| Gap | Why It Matters | Competitive Benchmark | Effort |
    90→|-----|---------------|----------------------|--------|
    91→| **Payroll API partnerships** | In-house payroll (US + India) exists but can't compete with Deel ($17.3B). API integration would extend to 100+ countries. | Deel, Remote, Papaya APIs. | 5-10 days |
    92→| **AI resume parsing** | Document management extracts data, but no dedicated resume parser for auto-filling profiles. | Most ATS platforms have this. | 2-3 days |
    93→| **Candidate CRM** | No talent pool/pipeline for nurturing candidates over time. | LinkedIn Recruiter, Greenhouse. | 5-7 days |
    94→| **White-label / API access** | No public API or embeddable widgets for partners. | Enterprise feature. Later. | 10+ days |
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## 4. Priority Roadmap — What to Build Next
    99→
   100→Based on competitive advantage × effort × user impact:
   101→
   102→### Phase 1: Deepen the Moat (Weeks 1-2)
   103→1. **OmniScore v2** — Company scoring, historical trends, explainable AI. This is the ONE thing nobody else has. Make it undeniable.
   104→2. **Email notifications** — Applications, offers, interviews, onboarding milestones. Table stakes that are currently missing.
   105→3. **Pricing page + Stripe checkout** — Can't monetize without it.
   106→
   107→### Phase 2: Enterprise Readiness (Weeks 3-4)
   108→4. **EU AI Act compliance dashboard** — Bias audit trails, risk classification, transparency reports. Beat the Aug 2026 deadline.
   109→5. **Calendar integration** — Google Calendar + Outlook for interview scheduling. Current manual scheduling is a dealbreaker.
   110→6. **Custom workflow builder** — Drag-and-drop pipeline stages. Enterprise expectation.
   111→
   112→### Phase 3: Polish & Scale (Weeks 5-8)
   113→7. **React SPA migration** — Kill legacy HTML. Unified frontend for faster iteration.
   114→8. **Interviewer evaluation** — Coach both sides of the interview. Unique positioning.
   115→9. **ATS integrations** — Start with Greenhouse API. Enterprise sales pipeline.
   116→10. **Mobile audit** — Ensure all flows work on mobile.
   117→
   118→### Phase 4: Expansion (Months 3+)
   119→11. **Payroll API partnerships** — Deel/Remote integration for global coverage.
   120→12. **AI resume parsing** — Auto-fill candidate profiles from uploaded resumes.
   121→13. **Candidate CRM** — Talent pools for long-term nurturing.
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Summary Scorecard
   126→
   127→| Category | Count | Details |
   128→|----------|-------|---------|
   129→| ✅ Fully built | 13 modules | Strong AI integration across assessments, interviews, matching, onboarding |
   130→| ⚠️ Partially built | 2 modules + frontend migration | Real-time collab, custom workflows, React migration |
   131→| ❌ Missing (high priority) | 4 capabilities | OmniScore depth, EU AI Act, integrations, email notifications |
   132→| ❌ Missing (medium priority) | 6 capabilities | Interviewer eval, AI job descriptions, status portal, mobile, pricing, calendar |
   133→| ❌ Missing (low priority) | 4 capabilities | Payroll APIs, resume parsing, CRM, white-label |
   134→
   135→**Bottom line:** The foundation is solid — 86% of modules are built with real AI. The gap isn't in breadth, it's in **depth** (OmniScore), **compliance** (EU AI Act), and **table-stakes features** (email, pricing, calendar) that every competitor already has.
   136→
   137→Build OmniScore v2 first. That's the moat.
   138→

## Complete HireLoop Application Architecture Documentation

### EXECUTIVE OVERVIEW

HireLoop is a comprehensive AI-native hiring platform built with Express.js backend and React frontend. The application spans 15 core modules covering recruitment, assessment, onboarding, payroll, and compliance. It features 19 API route files with 322 total endpoints and 47 database migrations representing a fully mature product.

**Current Status:** 13/15 modules fully built (86%), 2 partially complete (real-time collaboration, custom workflows)

---

## 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```

├── client/
│   └── src/
│       ├── pages/               (23 React pages)
│       │   ├── admin/
│       │   ├── candidate/
│       │   ├── recruiter/
│       │   └── debug/
│       ├── components/          (UI components + layout)
│       ├── contexts/            (Auth context)
│       ├── lib/                 (API client, utils)
│       └── App.tsx
├── routes/                      (19 API route files, 322 endpoints)
├── services/                    (14 business logic services)
├── lib/                         (9 core libraries)
├── public/                      (39 legacy HTML pages + CSS/JS)
├── migrations/                  (47 database migrations)
├── docs/                        (6 documentation files)
├── bin/                         (Audio/Piper for TTS)
└── server.js                    (Express app, 989 lines)
```
